mission.title: cortex: Switch Cortex Ruler to query Query Frontend
mission.description: Switch Cortex Ruler to query Query Frontend. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current cortex deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cortex version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cortex -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cortex
helm list -n cortex 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cortex installation.

steps.title: Review cortex configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cortex configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cortex -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cortex
kubectl get configmap -n cortex -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cortex
```
As we can see from the Cortex architecture https://cortexmetrics.io/docs/architecture/, Ruler queries ingesters (and store gateways) to get data for rules evaluation.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Switch Cortex Ruler to query Query Frontend
steps.description:
Add `ruler.frontend-address` to allow query to query frontends instead of ingesters. If `-ruler.frontend-address` is set, rulers query to query frontends for evaluating rules. It can support query frontend features like vertical query sharding.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cortex -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cortex
kubectl get events -n cortex --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Switch Cortex Ruler to query Query Frontend" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Add `ruler.frontend-address` to allow query to query frontends instead of ingesters. If `-ruler.frontend-address` is set, rulers query to query frontends for evaluating rules. It can support query frontend features like vertical query sharding.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cortex installed or the issue environment reproducible.
